sentences.text: The angel ___ offered €500,000 of his own personal money in exchange for a share of the company.
sentences.hint: A person who puts money into a business hoping to make a profit

sentences.text: The startup secured a seed ___ of €2 million, which would fund the first eighteen months of development.
sentences.hint: Money put into a business or project with the expectation of profit

sentences.text: The company generates most of its ___ from monthly subscription fees paid by users.
sentences.hint: The total income a business earns from its activities

sentences.text: The venture capital firm manages a ___ of twenty-three companies, mostly in fintech and healthcare.
sentences.hint: A collection of financial investments held by a person or company

sentences.text: The business ___ was risky, but the potential rewards were enormous if the product succeeded.
sentences.hint: A risky business project or activity undertaken for profit

sentences.text: The ___ was founded in a garage just two years ago but now employs over a hundred people.
sentences.hint: A newly established business, especially in technology

sentences.text: Before raising any money, the founders built a working ___ to show what the product could do.
sentences.hint: An early model of a product, built to test the concept

sentences.text: The founders sold 20% of their ___ — their ownership share in the company — during the first funding round.
sentences.hint: The value of ownership in a company, represented as shares

sentences.text: After the latest funding round, the company's ___ reached €50 million.
sentences.hint: An estimate of how much a company is worth

sentences.text: The founder had just five minutes to ___ his idea to a room full of investors.
sentences.hint: To present a business idea in order to persuade someone to invest

sentences.text: The venture capital firm acquired a 30% ___ in the company, giving them significant influence over major decisions.
sentences.hint: A share or financial interest in a business

sentences.text: Before signing the deal, the investors spent six weeks conducting ___, examining every financial record and contract.
sentences.hint: A thorough investigation of a business before making an investment

sentences.text: The fund delivered a 300% ___ on the original investment within just four years.
sentences.hint: The profit earned from an investment

sentences.text: The CEO spent three months on a ___ round, meeting over forty potential investors before closing the deal.
sentences.hint: The process of seeking financial support for a business or project

sentences.text: With a monthly ___ of €80,000 and only €400,000 in the bank, the startup had just five months of runway left.
sentences.hint: The speed at which a company spends its available money

sentences.text: After the latest investment, the company had enough ___ to operate for at least eighteen months without new funding.
sentences.hint: The amount of time a company can keep operating before it runs out of money

sentences.text: Investors want to see a ___ business model — one that can grow rapidly without costs increasing at the same rate.
sentences.hint: Able to grow or expand easily to handle increased demand

sentences.text: The app gained serious ___ after launch, reaching 50,000 active users in its first three months.
sentences.hint: Evidence that a product is gaining popularity and momentum in the market

sentences.text: The investors' preferred ___ strategy was to sell the company to a larger tech firm within five years.
sentences.hint: The way an investor gets their money back, usually by selling the company or going public

sentences.text: The company closed its Series A ___, raising €5 million from three different venture capital firms.
sentences.hint: A stage of funding in which a company raises money from investors
